name: Operations/import-search-2
description: Search-parameter indexing conformance suite, ported as the $import-independent equivalent of the Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E ImportCompositeSearchTests / ImportDateSearchTests / ImportNumberSearchTests / ImportQuantitySearchTests / ImportReferenceSearchTests / ImportStringSearchTests / ImportTokenSearchTests / ImportUriSearchTests suites. Those tests load fixtures via the $import operation (which requires Azure-Storage-backed ndjson blobs and an async poll-until-complete workflow the TestScript engine cannot orchestrate) and then assert that the imported resources are correctly searchable by a specific search-parameter type. Since $import's own mechanics are opaque plumbing and the property under test is really 'does the search index correctly reflect this resource shape', this suite creates the same resource shapes with standard client-supplied-id update (PUT) requests instead, then runs equivalent search assertions per search-parameter type. All fixtures share meta.tag http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/test|fhir262-impsrch-suite and every list-returning search scopes with _tag=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/test|fhir262-impsrch-suite to stay isolated from other suites' resources of the same type on a shared server. Part 2 of 2: reference, string, token, and uri search-parameter types. See import-search.json for composite, date, number, and quantity.

address.city: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit. Vestibulum quis molestie dui.

operation.description: PUT Patient ref-pat-1 (managingOrganization=bare id, no type prefix; param type is constrained to Organization)
PUT Patient ref-pat-3 (generalPractitioner=bare id, ambiguous type: Practitioner|Organization|PractitionerRole)
PUT Patient str-3 (long Lorem-ipsum city value containing 'Vestibulum', Murphy)
PUT Patient str-4 (Moscow, family contains a literal comma: 'Richard,Muller')
PUT Observation tok-3 (valueCodeableConcept coding system1|code2, display=text2)
PUT Observation tok-4 (valueCodeableConcept coding system3|code3, display=text)
PUT Observation tok-5 (text=text plus codings system1|code1 and system3|code2)
PUT Observation tok-6 (codings system2|code1 and system3|code3 display=text2)
PUT Observation tok-7 (valueCodeableConcept coding with no system, code3)

test.name: Reference: typed reference search (organization=Organization/{id})
test.description: Patient?organization=Organization/{id} matches on a typed managingOrganization reference. ref-pat-0 (managingOrganization=Organization/ref-org) must match; ref-pat-1 (untyped reference to a different id) must not.

assert.description: Must NOT include ref-pat-1 (different, untyped reference)

test.name: Reference: untyped resource reference is indexed under the parameter's constrained type
test.description: Patient?organization={id} matches ref-pat-1, whose managingOrganization reference omits the type prefix; the organization parameter is constrained to Organization so the engine must still index it.

test.name: Reference: general-practitioner typed reference and type-modifier bare id both resolve
test.description: Patient?general-practitioner=Practitioner/{id} and ?general-practitioner:Practitioner={id} are equivalent ways to match a typed reference. Both must match ref-pat-2.

assert.description: Type-modifier + bare id: must include ref-pat-2

test.name: Reference: untyped resource reference matches an untyped bare-id query
test.description: Patient?general-practitioner={id} matches ref-pat-3, whose generalPractitioner reference is untyped (ambiguous: Practitioner|Organization|PractitionerRole), when the query is also untyped.

test.name: Reference: type-modifier query against an untyped resource reference is a known ambiguous edge case
test.description: Patient?general-practitioner:Practitioner={id} against ref-pat-3 (whose reference has no type, and the param can resolve to Practitioner|Organization|PractitionerRole) is not guaranteed to match since the type is never resolved. Servers may vary here, hence warningOnly.

assert.description: Ambiguous typed match against an untyped reference is not expected to succeed

test.name: String: default modifier is a case-insensitive starts-with match
test.description: Patient?address-city=sea matches str-0 (Seattle) case-insensitively via starts-with; str-1 (Portland) must not match.

assert.description: Must include str-0 (Seattle starts with sea, case-insensitive)

test.name: String: :exact modifier requires a case-sensitive full match
test.description: Patient?address-city:exact=Seattle matches str-0; the same query with lowercase 'seattle' must not match since :exact is case-sensitive.

assert.description: Case-mismatch query: must NOT include str-0 (exact is case-sensitive)

test.name: String: :contains modifier matches a substring anywhere in the value
test.description: Patient?address-city:contains=att matches str-0 (Seattle contains 'att'); str-1 (Portland) must not match.

test.name: String: :contains matches a substring within a long descriptive value
test.description: Patient?address-city:contains=Vestibulum matches str-3, whose city is a long Lorem-ipsum value containing 'Vestibulum'; str-0 (Seattle) must not match.

assert.description: Must include str-3 (long value contains Vestibulum)

test.name: String: comma-separated values are ORed
test.description: Patient?family=Smith,Anderson matches str-0 (Smith) and str-2 (Anderson); str-1 (Williams) must not match.

test.name: String: repeating the name parameter ANDs across given and family
test.description: Patient?name=Bea&name=Smith requires one name token to match Bea and another to match Smith; only str-0 (given=Bea, family=Smith) satisfies both. str-2 (Anderson) must not match.

test.name: String: an escaped comma is treated as a literal character, not a value separator
test.description: Patient?name=Richard\,Muller (backslash-escaped comma) searches for the literal string 'Richard,Muller' as a single value, matching str-4's family name. str-0 (Smith) must not match.

test.name: Token: bare code matches the code in any system
test.description: Observation?value-concept=code1 (no system) matches any resource with a code1 coding regardless of system: tok-0, tok-5, tok-6. tok-1 (code2) must not match.

test.name: Token: system|code requires an exact system+code match
test.description: Observation?value-concept=system2|code2 matches only tok-1; tok-3 (system1|code2, same code but different system) must not match.

test.name: Token: |code (explicit empty system) matches only codings without a system
test.description: Observation?value-concept=|code3 matches tok-7 (coding has no system); tok-4 (system3|code3, has a system) must not match.

assert.description: Must include tok-7 (coding with no system, code3)
Must NOT include tok-4 (system3|code3, has a system)

test.name: Token: :text modifier matches CodeableConcept.text or a coding's display
test.description: Observation?value-concept:text=text2 matches tok-3 (coding display=text2) and tok-6 (coding display=text2); tok-2 (text=text, not text2) must not match.

test.name: Token: :not modifier excludes resources with a matching value
test.description: Observation?value-concept:not=code1 must NOT include tok-0, tok-5, or tok-6 (all have a code1 coding); it must include tok-1 (no code1 coding).

assert.description: Must NOT include tok-6 (has code1 in system2)

test.name: Token: :not modifier includes resources missing the searched value entirely
test.description: Observation?category:not=test must include tok-0 (no category element at all — a resource missing the parameter still satisfies :not); it must NOT include tok-8 (category=system|test).

assert.description: Must include tok-0 (no category at all)

test.name: Uri: exact match is case-sensitive
test.description: ValueSet?url=http://somewhere.com/test/system matches uri-0 exactly; the same url with a different case (somewhere.COM) must not match since uri values are case-sensitive.

assert.description: Case-mismatch query: must NOT include uri-0 (uri match is case-sensitive)

test.name: Uri: :above matches stored urls that are prefixes (ancestors) of the search value
test.description: ValueSet?url:above=http://somewhere.com/test/system/123 matches uri-0, whose url is a prefix of the (more specific) search value; uri-2 (unrelated url) must not match.

assert.description: Must include uri-0 (its url is a prefix of the search value)

test.name: Uri: :below matches stored urls that start with the (shorter) search value
test.description: ValueSet?url:below=http matches uri-0, uri-2, and uri-3 (all start with 'http'); uri-1 (urn scheme) must not match.
